_comment: Generic Claude Code settings for a research project. Copy to your project's .claude/settings.json and adapt.

permissions._comment_precedence: Precedence: deny > ask > allow. We never hard-BLOCK anything — every dangerous command is in 'ask' so Claude pauses and you approve (or refuse) in the moment. 'deny' is left empty by default; add a command there only if you want it blocked outright with no prompt.
permissions._comment_allow: Allow all Bash commands by default — no prompt for ordinary work: compile LaTeX, run Python/wolframscript, git, install packages, any standard research tool.
permissions._comment_ask: Dangerous-but-sometimes-wanted: PROMPT for confirmation instead of running silently. Nothing here is blocked — you can always approve inline when you really mean it (e.g. an intentional 'rm'). This is the safety net that catches Claude before an irreversible action without ever standing in your way.
permissions._comment_deny: Empty by default: nothing is hard-blocked. If you ever want a command refused outright with no prompt, add it here ('deny' overrides 'ask' and 'allow').

hooks._comment: Hooks run shell commands automatically at specific events. Test each hook manually before relying on it. Document every hook in CLAUDE.md so you can diagnose unexpected behaviour.

PreCompact._comment: Runs before Claude compresses the conversation context. Stamps CLAUDE.md and snapshots the task log so you never lose track of where you were.

PostToolUse._comment: After pushing to GitHub, automatically mirror to GitLab. Adjust matcher to your primary remote name. Remove this hook if you only have one remote.

Stop._comment: Catches 'I'll remember / I've noted / I've saved' said without actually writing a file — performative compliance. Prompts Claude to follow through. Adapted from flonat/claude-research.
